translation: Abraham begot Isaac; Isaac begot Jacob; and Jacob begot Judah and his brothers.

alternate_translations.translation: Abraham became the father of Isaac; Isaac became the father of Jacob; and Jacob became the father of Judah and his brothers.
alternate_translations.notes: The Greek verb ἐγέννησεν literally means “begot,” “fathered,” or “gave birth to.” Phrasing it as “became the father of” can read more naturally in English while keeping the same meaning.

notes: This is a genealogical formula from a Biblical genealogy (cf. Matthew 1:2). The names are transliterated directly (Ἀβραὰμ, Ἰσαάκ, Ἰακώβ, Ἰούδας). The phrase “Judah and his brothers” refers to Judah plus the other sons of Jacob.
